stocks.note: *Numbers as of market close on March 11th at 4:00 PM ET
stocks.news: Oracle (ORCL) stock rocketed higher after the company beat Wall Street's expectations on the top and bottom lines for its Q3 earnings on Tuesday and raised its 2027 revenue guidance to $90 billion. Shares jumped as much as 14% in early trading on Wednesday. Meanwhile, the broader market pulled back, with the S&P 500 falling 1.08%, the Dow dropping 1.34%, and the NASDAQ declining 1.49%.

economy.commentary:
The consumer price index increased a seasonally adjusted 0.3% for the month, putting the 12-month inflation rate at 2.4%. Core CPI posted a 0.2% monthly reading and 2.5% annual rate — all figures in line with Wall Street estimates. Rent rose just 0.1%, the smallest monthly increase since January 2021. Food prices accelerated 0.4% for the month and were up 3.1% from a year ago, though egg prices fell 3.8%.

WTI Crude Oil surged to $89.77 (+2.85%), adding to inflationary pressures across energy markets. Gold pulled back slightly to $5,153.00 (-0.67%) after its recent run-up, while silver eased to $85.65 (-0.47%). The 10-year Treasury yield rose to 4.24% (+0.10) as bond markets digested the latest inflation data.

crypto.commentary:
Bitcoin dipped 1.19% to $69,844.94, stabilizing near the $70K level as oscillators flash neutral signals. Ethereum held relatively steady, slipping just 0.21% to $2,059.55 as the ETH scarcity index turned positive. XRP eased 0.72% to $1.38.

Wells Fargo signaled a deeper push into crypto by filing a trademark for WFUSD. The move mirrors JPMorgan's similar trademark filing that foreshadowed the bank's introduction of tokenized deposits on Ethereum layer-2 network Base. Major banks entering the stablecoin space could accelerate institutional adoption of blockchain-based payment rails.

real_estate.homebuilder: Meritage Homes Corporation, the fifth-largest public homebuilder in the U.S., has scheduled its first-quarter 2026 earnings conference call for April 23.
real_estate.commentary:
The Bay Area housing market is entering its seasonal spring planning phase, characterized by steady but not overheating dynamics. In Santa Clara County, single-family homes in the $2M–$3M range remain highly competitive, while the condo and townhome segment is expected to remain flat or slightly down. Median home prices in the county were recently tracked at approximately $1.50M.

Alameda County continues to offer more affordability with a median sale price of roughly $960K, drawing buyers who are willing to tolerate longer commutes for increased value. Experts suggest that more inventory may hit the market in 2026 as homeowners look to capture capital gains before tax-free exclusion windows expire.

Latest General News in the Bay Area:

📰 In Milpitas, local ballot measures have drawn attention, including proposed amendments to the Municipal Code to establish stricter term limits for the mayor and councilmembers.

📰 In broader Santa Clara County, multiple school districts are focusing on fiscal stability through parcel tax renewals and bond authorizations, such as the $214 million bond measure for local facility improvements.

📰 Fremont and the surrounding Tri-City area are preparing for seasonal community events, while regional transit discussions continue to focus on long-term infrastructure funding through business-based taxes.
